definition_en: Feeling worried or nervous about something
example: She felt anxious before her job interview.

definition_en: Feeling annoyed because you cannot achieve something
example: He was frustrated that nobody listened to his ideas.

definition_en: Feeling unable to cope with too much work or emotion
example: She felt overwhelmed by all her responsibilities at work.

definition_en: Satisfied and happy with a situation
example: He was content with his quiet life in the countryside.

definition_en: A deep feeling of sadness, often without a clear reason
example: Looking at old photos filled her with melancholy.

definition_en: Feeling extreme happiness or excitement
example: She was ecstatic when she found out she had passed.

definition_en: Feeling unhappy because someone has something you want
example: He was envious of his colleague's promotion.

definition_en: Deep sadness, especially caused by someone's death
example: The whole community shared in her grief.

definition_en: Feeling uncomfortable or ashamed due to something awkward
example: He was embarrassed when he forgot her name.

definition_en: Feeling happy that a worry or difficulty has gone
example: She was relieved to hear the test results were normal.

definition_en: Feeling sad because you are away from home
example: He felt very homesick during his first month abroad.

definition_en: A feeling of bitterness or anger about unfair treatment
example: She carried a lot of resentment towards her former employer.

definition_en: A feeling of sympathy and care for others who are suffering
example: The nurse showed great compassion towards her patients.

definition_en: Feeling pleased about an achievement or quality
example: Her parents were very proud when she graduated.

definition_en: Not interested or concerned about something
example: He seemed completely indifferent to the criticism.

definition_en: Feeling a sentimental longing for the past
example: Looking at old photos made her feel nostalgic.

definition_en: The feeling of having done something wrong
example: He felt guilt for not visiting his parents more often.

definition_en: The feeling of being alone and unhappy about it
example: Loneliness is one of the biggest challenges in old age.

definition_en: Eager to know or learn about something
example: Children are naturally curious about the world around them.

definition_en: Worried or nervous about something that might happen
example: She was apprehensive about starting a new school.

definition_en: Extremely angry
example: He was furious when he found out he had been lied to.

definition_en: Feeling thankful for something someone has done
example: She was deeply grateful for all the support she received.
